§5.2 Null Spaces

Definition 5.2.1.

For a matrix , its null space is the set of vectors such that .

Example 5.2.2.

Fix matrix

find its null space.

Solution.

Notice the solution is any multiples of

Thus, the null space is a line in .

Remark.

Notice that the zero vector is always in the null space.

Theorem 5.2.3.

The null space is a subspace.

Proof.

We need to show that if and , then . Since , it suffices to show that .

Looked through the lens of linear systems, when there are more equations than there are variable, it is not generally true that for any , there are unique solutions. Thus, the null space describes the valid solutions.
